reasoning: While programmatic signals show high text recall, the LLM fidelity check identifies major structural and visual failures that compromise the document's utility. Specifically, the loss of 50% of the core data visualizations (rendered as empty blocks) and the incorrect nesting of multiple slides into a single section require human intervention to restore informational integrity.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is broken starting from Page 11. Slides 1 through 10 are correctly placed in their own sections. However, Slides 11 through 16 are merged into a single section (Section 11). The title of Slide 11 is used as the section title, while the titles of Slides 12, 13, 14, 15, and 16 are rendered as H2 headings within that section. This incorrectly nests top-level slide titles (Q9, Q10, Summary, etc.) as subsections of Slide 11 (Q8), which is a major structural nesting error.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Slide 12: Q9: If possible, provide evidence for how census is used for funding or services rates. (This is a slide title, equal in hierarchy to Slide 11's title)
concerns.output_quote: "type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "Q9: If possible, provide evidence for how census is used for funding or services rates.", "id": "heading-3"

concerns.analysis: The document contains several critical data visualizations (bar charts and a pie chart) that represent 50% of the survey's core findings. In the HTML output, every one of these charts (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q5, and Q7) is rendered as an empty solid green rectangle, completely losing all data points, labels, and axes. concerns.output_quote: The HTML rendering displays these charts as solid green rectangles with no data (e.g., appearing under the heading 'Q1: Which sector do you represent?').

action_items: Fix the heading hierarchy beginning at Slide 11 to ensure each slide title is a top-level heading.
Restore or re-embed the missing data visualizations/charts for survey questions Q1-Q7.
Repair the distorted organizational logo on the cover page.
